items.text:
CAMPUS-WIDE FIRE DRILL — TUESDAY, OCTOBER 15

A campus-wide fire drill will be conducted on Tuesday, October 15, beginning at 10:30 AM. All buildings on the main campus will participate, and the drill is expected to last approximately 45 minutes.

When the alarm sounds, occupants must follow these procedures:

• Exit the building immediately using the nearest stairwell
• Proceed to the assembly point posted at the main entrance
• Wait until the floor warden signals that re-entry is permitted
• Do not use elevators under any circumstances

Faculty teaching during the drill should pause class and lead students out together. Visitors must be escorted by their host. Anyone requiring evacuation assistance should notify the Office of Campus Safety in advance to be added to the accommodation list.

A $25 fine will be charged to any student found re-entering a building before the all-clear signal is given. Repeated violations may result in disciplinary review.

Questions about the drill should be directed to Campus Safety at safety@campus.edu or extension 4412.

— Office of Campus Safety

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should occupants do as soon as the alarm sounds?

options.A: To exit the building immediately using the nearest stairwell.
options.B: To gather their belongings before leaving the room.
options.C: To remain in their classrooms until further notice.
options.D: To wait for instructions from the floor warden by phone.

questions.explanation: The notice lists 'Exit the building immediately using the nearest stairwell' as the first required procedure when the alarm sounds.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To recruit new volunteers for the campus safety team.
options.B: To update visitors on parking restrictions during October.
options.C: To inform the campus community about an upcoming fire drill.
options.D: To announce changes to the building access policy.

questions.explanation: The notice's central purpose is to inform the campus community about the October 15 fire drill and the procedures to follow during it.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who need evacuation assistance?

options.A: They will be exempted from the $25 re-entry fine.
options.B: They must complete an online safety course beforehand.
options.C: They should contact Campus Safety prior to the drill.
options.D: They will not be allowed to participate in the drill.

questions.explanation: The notice states that anyone requiring evacuation assistance should notify the Office of Campus Safety in advance to be added to the accommodation list, implying they should contact Campus Safety beforehand.
